title: KDNA Fidelity Result
description: The output of a fidelity measurement run — measures whether domain judgment actually transferred into a generated artifact, not whether the output is stylistically good.

protocol_version.description: Semver of the Fidelity Protocol used for this measurement.

target_artifact.description: The artifact being measured for fidelity.

source_kdna.description: KDNA domains whose judgment transfer is being measured. Must match target_artifact.source_kdna.

overall_score.description: Aggregate fidelity score across all dimensions. 0 = no judgment transferred. 1 = complete judgment transfer identical to domain gold standard.

pass_threshold.description: Minimum overall_score required to pass. Default 0.70. Domains with higher risk levels may require higher thresholds.

dimensions.description: Per-dimension fidelity measurements. Each dimension measures a specific aspect of judgment transfer.

weight.description: Relative weight of this dimension in overall_score. All weights should sum to 1.0.

threshold.description: Minimum score for this dimension to pass individually. Used for hard-fail dimensions.

per_axiom.description: Per-axiom transfer fidelity. Maps each domain axiom to its measured transfer strength.

domain.description: Which KDNA domain this axiom belongs to.

transfer_level.description: Level of transfer. operationalized = axiom shapes decisions. referenced = axiom cited and applied. mentioned = axiom quoted but not operationalized. absent = axiom not detected. contradicted = output conflicts with axiom.

comparison.description: Results from controlled comparison: same task, same model, different judgment conditions.

condition_type.description: no_kdna = baseline without any judgment. best_prompt = optimized prompt without KDNA. kdna_loaded = KDNA domain loaded. kdna_cluster = multiple KDNA domains composed. calibration_positive = expected to score high. calibration_negative = expected to score low.

model_config.description: Temperature, top_p, and other generation parameters.

judgment_delta.description: Score difference from baseline (no_kdna). Positive = KDNA improved judgment transfer.

blind_delta.description: The measured difference between KDNA and Best Prompt in a blind evaluation. Positive = KDNA provides measurable value beyond the best prompt-only approach. This is the key metric for judging whether KDNA matters.

blind_design.description: Blind evaluation design. a_b_shuffled = two outputs randomly labeled A/B. a_b_c_shuffled = three outputs (no_kdna, best_prompt, kdna) randomly labeled A/B/C. pairwise = all pairs compared. ranking = all outputs ranked.

evaluator_model.description: Model used for blind evaluation (should differ from generation model).

evaluator_rubric.description: The evaluation rubric or prompt used for blind scoring.

calibration.description: Calibration anchor results. Verifies that the fidelity measurement itself is working correctly.

positive_anchor.description: A case expected to show high fidelity (domain gold standard scenario).

negative_anchor.description: A case expected to show low fidelity (domain explicitly does not apply).

calibration_valid.description: Whether both anchors passed. If false, the fidelity result should not be trusted.

cross_model.description: Cross-model consistency: same KDNA, same task, different models. Measures whether judgment transfer is model-dependent.

cross_model_variance.description: Standard deviation of scores across models. Low variance = judgment transfers consistently regardless of model.

tasks.description: The evaluation tasks used for measurement. Each task tests judgment transfer from a different angle.

task_type.description: core = normal application. boundary = domain does not apply. failure = misunderstanding triggered. excluded = edge case. novel = unseen variation. adversarial = designed to confuse.

trace_refs.description: Links to KDNA trace records from the measured generation.

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when measurement completed.
